title: secretx-k8s: crate scaffold and workspace wiring
description:
What: Create the secretx-k8s crate manifest, empty src/lib.rs skeleton, and integrate the crate into the Cargo workspace so all subsequent beads compile against a real package.

Algorithm:
1. Add to workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]: kube = {version="0.91", features=["client","config","rustls-tls"]}, k8s-openapi = {version="0.27", features=["v1_32"]}.
2. Add "secretx-k8s" to workspace Cargo.toml members array (alphabetically between secretx-keyring and secretx-local-signing).
3. Add secretx-k8s = {path="secretx-k8s", version="0.3.0"} to [workspace.dependencies].
4. Create secretx-k8s/Cargo.toml: package fields (name, version.workspace, edition.workspace, rust-version.workspace, license.workspace, authors.workspace, description, readme, repository). Dependencies: secretx-core={workspace=true}, async-trait={workspace=true}, inventory={workspace=true}, zeroize={workspace=true, features=["alloc"]}, tokio={workspace=true, features=["rt"]}, kube={workspace=true}, k8s-openapi={workspace=true}, serde_json={workspace=true}. Dev-dependencies: tokio={workspace=true, features=["macros","rt-multi-thread"]}.
5. Create secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs with only a module-level doc comment and no logic.
6. Run: cargo check -p secretx-k8s.

Defensive programming rules:
- No implementation logic in this bead — manifests and empty file only.
- Do not add kube/k8s-openapi deps to any other existing crate's Cargo.toml.
- Verify workspace integrity with cargo check before declaring done.

Subagent instructions: Single subagent. Read secretx-keyring/Cargo.toml and workspace Cargo.toml before writing manifests to match the exact workspace field patterns. Run cargo check -p secretx-k8s to validate.

Tests: cargo check -p secretx-k8s exits 0. cargo metadata --workspace shows secretx-k8s as a member.
